proof_sketch: The mutation changes the boundary guard from `>=` to `>`, allowing a thread with `output_pos == output_length` to execute. This thread writes to `output[output_idx]` where `output_idx` includes `output_length`, which is one past the last valid element. The output tensor is allocated with exactly `output_length` elements, so this write is out-of-bounds and does not affect any element of the returned tensor. The input reads are bounded, so no value difference propagates to the output. Thus the outputs remain bitwise identical regardless of input values.

The input reads are bounded, so no value difference propagates to the output. Thus the outputs remain bitwise identical regardless of input values.",

All previous testing strategies attempted to find input values that produce different output values, but since the defect does not propagate to the output, no input can expose it under the fixed-shape contract. For test coverage, ensure that the kernel is tested with configurations where `output_length` is a multiple of the block size and where it is not, and use memory-checking tools to detect out-of-bounds writes."
}
